title: 11 Herbal Teas For Chapped Lips
intro_desc: If you're dealing with chapped lips, herbal teas can be a great solution. These teas work by providing moisture to the skin and helping to heal any irritation. For example, Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe dry, cracked lips. It helps to lock in moisture and protect the skin from further drying out.  Another herb that's often used for its soothing properties is Echinacea purpurea. This herb is known for its ability to calm and protect the skin, making it an excellent choice for chapped lips. Echinacea also has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent infection and speed up the healing process.  Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, is also a popular choice for herbal teas that target chapped lips. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling, while its moisturizing properties can help to lock in hydration and soothe dry skin. Drinking these teas can bring several benefits to your life. Not only will your lips feel better, but you'll also be rehydrating your body, which can lead to a range of other health benefits.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent chapped lips. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have soothing properties that can keep your lips moisturized. These teas can also help reduce dryness and inflammation caused by cold weather or dry air, which can lead to chapped lips.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent chapped lips from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have soothing properties that can keep your lips moisturized. They can also help calm dry skin, which can reduce the risk of chapped lips. This is because herbal teas often contain antioxidants that support skin health.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for chapped lips are generally safe to consume every day. However, some may cause allergic reactions or interact with medications. Look for teas made from soothing herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, or peppermint. Start with small amounts and observe how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for chapped lips can start working within a few days to a week. Some people notice improvements in lip moisture and smoothness after 2-3 days of regular drinking, but it might take up to a week for the full benefits to show.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for chapped lips can be beneficial at night before bed. Many herbs, such as aloe vera and chamomile, have soothing properties that can calm and moisturize dry, chapped lips while you sleep. This allows the tea's benefits to work overnight, helping to heal and soften your lips by morning.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, but this is rare. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and ginger, have properties that might affect how your body uses certain medications. If you're taking prescription medications for chapped lips, it's best to check the labels of any herbal teas you drink.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some ingredients can affect your baby's health. If you want to try a herbal tea for chapped lips, choose one with well-known and safe ingredients like aloe vera or chamomile. Be sure to check the ingredients and follow any instructions carefully.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing remedy for chapped lips, but while breastfeeding, it's essential to be cautious. Some herbs can pass into breast milk, affecting the baby. Look for teas that are known to be safe during breastfeeding, like peppermint or chamomile. Always check the ingredients and follow the recommended amounts to minimize any potential risks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis teas contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and protect chapped lips. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have a strong ability to reduce inflammation and fight free radicals, making it easier for the skin to heal. The carotenoids, including beta-carotene, have antioxidant properties that help repair damaged skin cells and promote collagen production. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin repair, Calendula officinalis teas can help to heal and moisturize chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Calendula flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a separate container using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey to the cooled tea for sweetness and soothing properties.
Apply the tea to your chapped lips using a cotton swab or your finger, 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and redness associated with chapped lips, making Echinacea purpurea a popular natural remedy for this issue. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea also act as antioxidants, protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promoting healing. The soothing and calming effects of Echinacea purpurea's polysaccharides and glycosides can help to reduce irritation and discomfort in chapped lips, making it easier to recover from dry, cracked skin. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea may help to stimulate the production of new skin cells, promoting the healing process and leaving lips feeling smoother and healthier.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the dried flowers in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the flowers. Let it cool to room temperature.
Soak a cotton swab in the cooled tea and apply it to your chapped lips as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains glycyrrhizin and other active constituents that help soothe chapped lips. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and redness associated with chapped lips. The tea also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has moisturizing properties that can help lock in moisture and keep the skin hydrated, reducing the dryness and cracking of chapped lips. By drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra teas or applying it topically, glycyrrhizin and other active constituents can help to calm and heal chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Let the liquid cool to room temperature, then refrigerate it.
To use, dip a cotton swab in the cooled liquid and apply it to your chapped lips 2-3 times a day.
Repeat the process every day or as needed to keep your lips moisturized and healthy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas contains a range of bioactive constituents, including vitamins, minerals, and amino acids, that help to soothe and heal chapped lips. One of the key compounds found in aloe is glycoprotein, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling in the skin. The tea also contains acemannan, a complex carbohydrate that has been found to have wound-healing properties, helping to repair and protect dry, cracked skin. Additionally, the tea contains antioxidants like vitamin E and beta-carotene, which can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote healthy cell growth. By incorporating aloe barbadensis tea into your skincare routine, you can help to lock in moisture and promote healthy, hydrated skin on your lips.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Cut a 2-inch piece of Aloe barbadensis leaf and peel off the skin.
Chop the inner gel into small pieces and place them in a cup.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup and let it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to soothe chapped lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas contains rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps to soothe and protect dry, chapped lips. This compound also has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips. Rosmarinic acid also helps to lock in moisture, keeping lips hydrated and soft. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause irritation and infection on the lips. By drinking Rosmarinus officinalis tea, you can reap the benefits of its active constituents and promote healthy, smooth lips.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool down.
Strain the liquid to remove the leaves. Discard the leaves.
Add honey (optional) to the tea to sweeten it. Stir well.
Use the tea as a lip balm by applying it to your chapped lips using a cotton swab.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas contains active constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm chapped lips. The antiseptic and antimicrobial properties of linalool and linalyl acetate also help prevent infections and promote wound healing on the lips. Furthermore, the antioxidant properties of these compounds protect the lips from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and chapping. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas may help to keep the lips hydrated and healthy.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it reaches boiling point.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Let the tea cool down, then soak a cotton swab in it and apply to chapped lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis teas contains rosmarinic acid and apigenin, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These compounds help to reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips, promoting a comfortable and calm environment. Rosmarinic acid also has antioxidant properties, which can protect the delicate skin on the lips from damage caused by free radicals. Apigenin has a sedative effect, which can help to calm and relax the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort. By drinking Melissa officinalis teas, you may find that your chapped lips heal faster and feel more soothed.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to 1 cup of boiling water and stir.
Steep 1/4 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the honey water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and let it cool.
Soak a cotton swab in the cooled tea and apply it to your chapped lips for relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas contains menthol, menthone, and limonene as its key bioactive constituents. These compounds give peppermint tea its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and heal chapped lips. The antiseptic properties of menthol and menthone help to kill bacteria that can cause infection and irritation, reducing redness and swelling. The anti-inflammatory properties of limonene can also help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the skin on the lips. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, peppermint tea can help to calm and repair chapped lips, promoting healthy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh peppermint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and wash them with water.
Put the leaves in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. If you don't have one, simply bruise the leaves with a spoon to release their oils.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the peppermint leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Let the tea cool down to room temperature.
Soak a cotton swab in the cooled tea and apply it to your chapped lips. Repeat as needed for relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum teas contains silymarin, a mixture of flavonoids and polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help soothe and protect chapped lips by reducing inflammation and fighting off free radicals that can cause damage. The flavonoids in silymarin, particularly silybin, have been shown to have a protective effect on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and prevent dryness. The antioxidant properties of silymarin also help to repair and regenerate skin cells, promoting healthy lip tissue. By drinking Silybum marianum teas, you may see an improvement in the health and appearance of your chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Silybum marianum flowers and leaves.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Silybum marianum flowers and leaves in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into another cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea for taste. Stir well.
Let the tea cool down. Apply the tea to your chapped lips using a cotton swab.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis teas contains flavonoids, like theaflavin and thearubigin, and saponins, such as camelliasaponin A. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm chapped lips. The flavonoids also possess antioxidant properties, which can protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the saponins in Camellia sinensis teas have emollient properties, which can help to moisturize and hydrate dry, chapped lips. This combination of properties can help to promote healthy, smooth lips by reducing inflammation and locking in moisture.
remedies.image_alt: Camellia sinensis herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Camellia sinensis leaves.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a bowl to remove the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea to help soothe chapped lips.
Let the tea cool down before applying it to your chapped lips with a cotton swab.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains compounds like flavonoids and tannins, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to soothe and calm chapped lips by reducing redness and swelling. The tea also contains eucalyptol, a compound that has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healing in dry, cracked skin. Eucalyptus globulus teas has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Drinking eucalyptus globulus tea can help to keep the skin hydrated from the inside out, reducing the need for external moisturizers.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 2 tablespoons of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves, and a clean cup.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Eucalyptus leaves in the tea infuser or muslin bag.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into the clean cup.
Let the tea cool down, then use it to soothe chapped lips by applying it with a cotton swab or a clean cloth.
